output:
Implemented batch 8. T8 adds `supabase/functions/discord-media-importer/index.ts` with job claiming, Discord URL refresh, Storage/Cloudflare dispatch, media row creation, junction upserts, and retry/backoff handling. T18 adds the subtle ResourceCard Discord badge and factors the channel-name mapping into a shared helper used by both the card and the detail page.

Verification: `banodoco-website` `npm test -- --run` passed, `npm run build` passed, and repo-wide `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` still ends with the same 7 unrelated failures previously observed in this workspace. `deno check` could not be run because `deno` is not installed here.
commands_run: psql "$DB_URL" -Atc "select column_name, data_type, is_nullable, coalesce(column_default,'') from information_schema.columns where table_schema='public' and table_name='media' order by ordinal_position;"

psql "$DB_URL" -Atc "select column_name, data_type, is_nullable, coalesce(column_default,'') from information_schema.columns where table_schema='public' and table_name='asset_media' order by ordinal_position;"
psql "$DB_URL" -Atc "select classification, count(*) from public.media group by classification order by count(*) desc nulls last;"
psql "$DB_URL" -Atc "select source, count(*) from public.media group by source order by count(*) desc nulls last;"
deviations: T8 uses the exact `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` claim query when `SUPABASE_CONNECTION_STRING`/`SUPABASE_DB_URL`/`DATABASE_URL` is available, and falls back to per-row Supabase REST claiming otherwise so the function still runs without a direct Postgres connection string.

The importer mirrors the repo's existing Cloudflare copy-ingest path by uploading Discord files into Supabase Storage first and then creating a Cloudflare Stream copy job for video/GIF media; it does not use tus upload because the checked-in Cloudflare code path does not.
`deno` is not installed in this environment, so the new edge function could not be type-checked with `deno check` or exercised with local Deno tooling.
Live staging verification for the importer remains blocked by the earlier staging schema gap from T6/T7.

task_updates.executor_notes: Implemented `supabase/functions/discord-media-importer/index.ts`. The function secures itself with the service-role bearer, claims up to 10 jobs, reclaims stale `in_progress` jobs, refreshes Discord attachment URLs only when the signed `?ex=` URL has expired, uploads attachments into `user-uploads/discord-imports/...`, sends video/GIF jobs to Cloudflare Stream without polling, inserts `media` rows with Discord provenance metadata plus `classification='discord-comment'` for comment media and `classification='submission'` for OP media, upserts the correct junction row, marks jobs `done` with `media_id`, and applies exponential backoff with permanent `failed` after 5 attempts. Verification here was static plus suite-level: `npm test -- --run` passed, `npm run build` passed, and repo-wide pytest still shows the same 7 unrelated failures. `deno check` could not run because Deno is unavailable, and the function was not exercised against staging because the earlier Discord-sync schema is still absent there.
commands_run: psql "$DB_URL" -Atc "select column_name, data_type, is_nullable, coalesce(column_default,'') from information_schema.columns where table_schema='public' and table_name='media' order by ordinal_position;"

psql "$DB_URL" -Atc "select column_name, data_type, is_nullable, coalesce(column_default,'') from information_schema.columns where table_schema='public' and table_name='asset_media' order by ordinal_position;"
psql "$DB_URL" -Atc "select classification, count(*) from public.media group by classification order by count(*) desc nulls last;"
psql "$DB_URL" -Atc "select source, count(*) from public.media group by source order by count(*) desc nulls last;"

task_updates.executor_notes: Implemented the subtle ResourceCard Discord badge. `banodoco-website/src/pages/Resources/ResourceCard.tsx` now shows a small `From Discord · <channel>` pill when `asset.source === 'discord_import'`, using a new shared helper at `src/lib/discordResources.ts`. `ResourceDetail/index.tsx` was updated to consume the same helper so the channel labels stay aligned between the detail view and the card view. Verification: `npm test -- --run` passed, `npm run build` passed, and repo-wide pytest still reports the same 7 unrelated failures.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Implemented in code with one runtime caveat. The importer now dispatches by content type, writes the expected media metadata/classification, upserts the correct junction table, marks jobs done with `media_id`, logs through `logger_name='discord_media_importer'`, and backs off to `failed` after 5 attempts. It uses the exact `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` claim query when a direct Postgres connection string is available, but includes a REST fallback when that connection string is absent. The function was not exercised end-to-end on staging in this environment.

Yes in code. `ResourceCard.tsx` now renders a small `From Discord · <channel>` pill when `asset.source === 'discord_import'`, and the channel label comes from the same shared helper used by the detail surface so the wording stays aligned.
